metadata.title: Computing Workforce Tiers by Country
metadata.description: Join table for computing workforce tier estimates (2024). Geometries are null — join to a world boundary layer using iso_numeric (ISO 3166-1 numeric, matches world-atlas/D3/Natural Earth) or iso3_alpha (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3, matches most GIS boundary files). Tier 1 = computer literacy required (basic use). Tier 2 = computational proficiency required (advanced use, non-ICT profession). Tier 3 = computing profession (ICT specialist, formal computing credential expected). Jobs in millions. Percentages of total employed workforce.

join_keys.primary: iso_numeric — ISO 3166-1 numeric (string, zero-padded to 3 digits). Matches world-atlas countries-110m.json feature id, D3 topojson, QGIS Natural Earth numeric field.
join_keys.secondary: iso3_alpha  — ISO 3166-1 alpha-3. Matches Natural Earth ADM0_A3, SOV_A3 fields and most national boundary datasets.

tier_definitions.Tier 1: Computer literacy required — routine digital tool use (email, office software, web, ERP). Computing supports the role but is not the technical core.
tier_definitions.Tier 2: Computational proficiency required — advanced analytics, GIS, modelling, scripting, or technical software. Computing is a significant skill but not the primary professional credential.
tier_definitions.Tier 3: Computing profession required — computing is the primary professional function; formal computing qualification conventionally expected (software engineer, data scientist, cybersecurity, cloud, AI/ML, database, network, GIS developer).

confidence_levels.Direct: National statistical office data used directly (e.g., IMDA, BLS, Eurostat, NASSCOM).
confidence_levels.Model (high-income): Estimated from ITU internet penetration + GDP/capita band + regional benchmarks. Uncertainty ±5pp.
confidence_levels.Model (low): Structural proxy estimate. Uncertainty ±12pp or more.

metadata.sources: Eurostat 2023-24; IMDA 2024; BLS 2023; ITU Facts & Figures 2023-24; ILO ILOSTAT 2023; World Bank WDI 2022-23; NASSCOM 2023; DOSM Malaysia 2023; NBS China; HSE Russia 2023; MCIT Saudi Arabia; TRA UAE; StatCan 2023; MIC Japan; MSIT Korea; IBGE Brazil; INEGI Mexico; Stats SA.
